records.ety: [Dan. kravle, or Icel. krafla, to paw, scrabble with the hands; akin to Sw. kräla to crawl; cf. LG. krabbeln, D. krabbelen to scratch.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crawled (krld); p. pr. & vb. n. Crawling.]

defs: To move slowly by drawing the body along the ground, as a worm; to move slowly on hands and knees; to creep.
quotes: A worm finds what it searches after only by feeling, as it crawls from one thing to another.

defs: to move or advance in a feeble, slow, or timorous manner.
quotes: He was hardly able to crawl about the room.
The meanest thing that crawl'd beneath my eyes.

defs: To advance slowly and furtively; to insinuate one's self; to advance or gain influence by servile or obsequious conduct.
quotes: Secretly crawling up the battered walls.
Hath crawled into the favor of the king.
Absurd opinions crawl about the world.

defs: To have a sensation as of insect creeping over the body; as, the flesh crawls. See Creep, v. i., 7.
The act or motion of crawling; slow motion, as of a creeping animal.
A pen or inclosure of stakes and hurdles on the seacoast, for holding fish.
